pronunciationGuide.desc: Nasal 'o', like French 'on'. Before b/p becomes 'om'.
Soft 'ch', like 'cheese' but softer. Tongue touches palate.
Nasal 'e', like French 'in'. At word end often just /ɛ/.
Soft 'sh', tongue near palate. Softer than 'sz'.
Hard 'zh', like 'pleasure'. ż and rz sound the same.
Like Scottish 'loch'. ch and h sound the same.
Between 'i' and 'u'. Like 'i' in 'bit' but further back.

n0.en: This woman is reading a book.

n1.en: This man works in an office.

n2.en: The child is playing in the park.

n4.en: The boy is eating an apple.

n6.en: The father is reading a newspaper.

n10.en: The dog is running in the park.

n11.en: The cat is sleeping on the couch.

n14.en: The fish swims in the river.

n30.en: I live in a big house.

n40.en: The tree grows in the garden.

n60.en: The keys are on the table.

v1.en: I have a lot of work.

v2.en: I like to eat Polish food.

v5.en: I'm going by train to Warsaw.

v10.en: I want to buy a new car.

v20.en: I've been learning Polish for a year.

a1.en: The little cat sleeps on the bed.

conversations.title: Meeting Someone
conversations.scene: You're at a party in Warsaw.

lines.speaker: other
lines.en: Nice to meet you! Where are you from?

lines.speaker: you
lines.pl: Jestem z Anglii. Uczę się polskiego.
lines.en: I'm from England. I'm learning Polish.

conversations.title: At a Cafe
conversations.scene: You walk into a cafe in Kraków.

lines.speaker: other
lines.en: Good day! What can I get you?

lines.speaker: you
lines.en: Good day. I'd like a coffee, please.

conversations.title: Asking for Directions
conversations.scene: You're lost in the city center.

lines.speaker: you
lines.en: Excuse me, where is the station?

lines.speaker: other
lines.pl: Proszę iść prosto, potem w lewo.

conversations.title: At the Market
conversations.scene: You're shopping at a local market.

lines.speaker: you
lines.en: Good day. How much are the apples?

conversations.title: Dinner at a Restaurant
conversations.scene: You're dining at a Polish restaurant.

lines.speaker: other
lines.pl: Dobry wieczór! Stolik dla dwóch osób?
lines.en: Good evening! A table for two?

lines.speaker: you
lines.pl: Tak, proszę. Mogę prosić o menu?
lines.en: Yes, please. May I have the menu?

lines.speaker: you
lines.en: I'd like pierogi and tomato soup.

conversations.title: At the Doctor
conversations.scene: You visit a doctor because you feel ill.

lines.speaker: other
lines.en: Good day. What seems to be the problem?

lines.speaker: you
lines.pl: Nie czuję się dobrze. Boli mnie głowa.
lines.en: I don't feel well. My head hurts.

lines.speaker: you
lines.en: Since yesterday. And I have a fever.

conversations.title: Checking into a Hotel
conversations.scene: You arrive at your hotel in Gdańsk.

lines.speaker: you
lines.en: Good day. I have a reservation.

lines.speaker: other
lines.pl: Od siódmej do dziesiątej. Miłego pobytu!
lines.en: From seven to ten. Enjoy your stay!

lines.speaker: other
lines.en: Hi! What are you doing tonight?

lines.speaker: you
lines.pl: Jeszcze nie wiem. Masz jakiś pomysł?
lines.en: I don't know yet. Do you have an idea?

lines.speaker: other
lines.en: Maybe we'll go to the cinema?

lines.speaker: other
lines.pl: O dwudziestej. Spotkamy się przed kinem.
lines.en: At eight. We'll meet in front of the cinema.

phrases.note: Pan=to a man, Pani=to a woman

phrases.pl: Jak to się mówi po polsku?
phrases.en: How do you say this in Polish?

phrases.note: When toasting or after a sneeze
On a bus, in a café

sentence_exercises.en: I like Polish food
My name is Anna
sentence_exercises.grammar: Fixed expression: Mam na imię + name

sentence_exercises.en: I live in a big house
The dog runs in the park
I want to buy a new car
The cat sleeps on the couch
I'm going to school
I drink coffee every morning
This woman is reading a book
My mother is cooking dinner
Where is the nearest shop?
I don't feel well
I've been learning Polish for a year
May I ask for the menu?
I'd like to reserve a table for two
sentence_exercises.grammar: Conditional + Infinitive + Object + for + two (gen.) + people (gen.)

sentence_exercises.en: Tomorrow it will rain
sentence_exercises.grammar: Tomorrow + will be + to fall + rain

sentence_exercises.en: He works in a big company
Can you help me?
sentence_exercises.grammar: Question particle + can you + me (dat.) + help (inf.)

sentence_exercises.en: I really like it here
I have to go to the doctor
